name: Justilin Foimi
description: Justilin Foimi is one of the most accomplished long-distance runners in Central and West Africa. Known for his consistency, endurance, and winning mentality, he has built a strong reputation across international marathons and road races. His ability to perform over a range of distances, from 5,000m to the full marathon, makes him a versatile and valuable athlete for elite competitions worldwide. Currently focused on advancing to higher-level international races, Justilin aims to compete on major global stages and continue improving his performance.

achievements.title: 6× Cameroon National Champion — 10,000m
achievements.subtitle: Dominant long-distance track performance at national level

name: Geofrey Kipchirchir Kirwa
description: Geofrey Kipchirchir Kirwa is a Kenyan long-distance runner based in Eldoret, Kenya. Specializing in the half marathon and marathon, he trains under Coach Mohammed Serem and has built a reputation as a strong road racing athlete throughout East Africa. Geofrey has recorded an official World Athletics verified half marathon personal best of 1:03:39 and a marathon best of 2:18:14. He is a proven competitor with victories and podium finishes in major regional races, including a victory at the Madiba Half Marathon. His ambition is to continue improving toward world-class marathon standards while expanding his international racing opportunities.

name: Alfred Kimani
description: Alfred Kimani is a Kenyan long-distance runner specializing in the 10,000m, 10K road races, and half marathon. Based in Nyahururu, Kenya, he trains in a competitive high-altitude environment alongside national and international athletes. A member of the Kenya Defence Forces athletic system, Alfred combines military discipline with strong work ethic and consistent training. His personal bests include 28:42 for 10K and 1:03:27 for the half marathon. Alfred's goal is to break 28 minutes for 10K and 60 minutes for the half marathon while building an international racing career.

Building an international professional running career

name: Sharon Jemaiyo Kipchumba
description: Sharon Jemaiyo Kipchumba is a Kenyan long-distance runner based in Iten, Kenya. Specializing in the marathon and half marathon, she trains under Coach Daniel Ngetich at Imani Training Camp. Sharon has competed internationally across Africa and Europe, including races in Kenya, Nigeria, Ghana, Turkey, and the United Kingdom. Her breakthrough performances include winning the women's division at the Wilmslow Half Marathon in England and finishing 10th at the Nairobi City Marathon in a personal best of 2:30:10. Sharon continues to pursue international racing opportunities with the goal of becoming one of Kenya's leading marathon athletes.
